description: The point of entering or beginning; the place of crossing between spaces. 'Maewo' names the liminal—the doorway where one realm meets another, the moment of crossing.
sound_symbolism: The word crosses over. 'Ma' grounds at the entrance, position marked. The vowel 'e' opens to crossing. Then 'wo' rounds into the new space, threshold passed.
grammatical_notes: Functions as a noun: 'lo maewo' (the threshold). Liminal space—neither fully inside nor outside, the place of transition.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Thresholds were guarded—doorways as vulnerable points requiring protection, blessing, or ritual.
pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Life is lived at thresholds—the constant crossing from one moment, one state, one understanding to the next.
